title: Minimized profile surface lighting
abstract: An EM lighting system includes a mounting assembly configured for mounting on a host structure above a ceiling, and a lighting module for attachment to the mounting assembly, including a lighting unit having a mounting side configured for attachment to the mounting assembly approximately flush with the ceiling. An electronics module supported by the mounting assembly includes an onboard battery and circuitry that powers the lighting module by use of the battery upon detecting a power failure. The lighting unit has a predefined depth extending from the ceiling, defining a low profile system. The mounting assembly is generally occluded above a ceiling. A ring bezel circumferentially surrounds the lighting unit and opens at least in the direction of the an illuminating side thereof. The bezel has a downward facing annular ring by which the illuminating side is offset to protect same from incidental contact in handling, packaging, installation, and use.
claims: 1. A lighting system comprising: (a) a mounting assembly configured for mounting on a host structure proximal to a first side of a mounting surface wherein the mounting surface is a ceiling portion or a wall and the host structure is configured to accommodate human inhabitants; (b) a lighting unit coupled to the mounting assembly, wherein the lighting unit comprises a mounting side configured for attachment to the mounting assembly approximately flush with a second side of the mounting surface; and (c) a mounting flange, wherein (i) the mounting flange is coupled to the mounting assembly proximal to the mounting surface, (ii) a portion of the mounting surface is captured between the mounting assembly and the mounting flange, and (iii) the mounting flange is planar with a top surface that frictionally engages the mounting surface and a bottom surface that frictionally engages the lighting unit.
